title: achillea millefolium subsp. setacea
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. setacea, also known as Yarrow, is a subspecies of the Yarrow plant, belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and white or yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Yarrow include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and easing digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming Yarrow include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce menstrual cramps, and improve respiratory health. Yarrow can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and salve, and is often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
